page: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this configuration provides 6 vCPUs across 3 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and features a large 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 252.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark results highlight strong single-core capabilities and specialized instruction set performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression at 34.92 GB/sec and Geekbench single-core testing with a score of 2350.0, alongside strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.31 GB/sec. The CPU supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in workloads like low-latency text processing and small-scale LLM inference, where it achieves 257.16 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model. However, multi-core performance is more moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7274.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 6664.64, reflecting the lower physical core count. Large-block memory bandwidth is measured at 50.68 GB/sec for read operations and 36.24 GB/sec for write operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX12-6ms_v2 offers a highly dense memory profile relative to its compute allocation, making it a specialized choice for workloads that require massive RAM footprints without needing high parallel core counts. This resource distribution is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, in-memory analytics, and text processing applications. Conversely, the low core count and lack of local storage or GPU acceleration make it less suitable for highly parallelized compute tasks, large-scale machine learning training, or high-throughput local I/O operations.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 6 vCPUs (3 physical cores) operating at 4.2 GHz, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and 252.0 GB of RAM. This configuration delivers an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 42.0 GB. While the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it excels in single-core performance, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench single-core tests and single-threaded gzip compression. Hardware-assisted acceleration is provided via AVX-512 and AMX extensions. The FX12-6ms_v2 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications that do not require massive parallel processing, making it highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 6 vCPUs, 252 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz, designed for memory-intensive workloads and database caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 6 vCPUs, 252 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 4.2 GHz CPU, ideal for in-memory databases.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual instance featuring high single-core clock speeds and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 260 MB L3 cache
252.0 GB of RAM providing a high 42.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and text processing
Supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX hardware acceleration
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-bound tasks
